$comment: Cold-case registry — every past prod/user-facing bug and the guard that keeps it dead. Policy: docs/qa-playbook.md. Guards are verified to exist by scripts/qa-registry-check.mjs (CI). A guard that fails = the case is REOPENED — louder than a new bug. READ .qa/learnings.md FIRST on every QA run — it holds the structural lessons behind these cases; every new cold case needs a learning entry there (CI-enforced).
bars_rationale: coverage_branch applies to src/lib (the vitest-enforced surface). coverage_branch_s1 applies to unit-VISIBLE branches of s1_paths files; recalibrated 0.8→0.55 on 2026-07-23 (QA-lawnbizops-20260723) because the original 0.8 was aspirational and contradicted the repo's documented coverage philosophy (vite.config: read hooks/route screens are e2e-covered, invisible to v8) — 0.8 was never met by any commit. Measured at recalibration: invoices 67.3, estimates 57.5, outbox 80.3. Ratchet up as write-path tests land. mutation_s1 is the covered-line Stryker score on s1_paths files (invoices 74.9, estimates 73.9 at recalibration); both money files are now in the CI-gated stryker scope.

cold_cases.title: Prod rendered Not Found on every route — router had no basepath under /lawnbizops/
cold_cases.root_cause: dev/e2e/preview all ran at base '/', so nothing exercised the GitHub Pages sub-path; latent since first deploy

cold_cases.title: Invoice creation broke in prod — numbering triggers still keyed business_settings by dropped user_id after org re-key
cold_cases.root_cause: migration 0012 re-keyed business_settings to org_id but the 0006/0007 numbering triggers kept `on conflict (user_id)`

cold_cases.title: Fully reversed invoice stuck at 'paid' — apply_payment recompute never reverted status
cold_cases.root_cause: status case ended in `else status`; correct for forward-only payments, wrong once offsetting negative lines exist

cold_cases.title: Duplicate estimate→invoice conversion could double-bill a client
cold_cases.root_cause: client-side Convert guard can't see the window between two offline devices or a retry racing the cache; no DB backstop

cold_cases.title: Two branches both claimed migration 0027 — hand-renumbered to 0031 at merge
cold_cases.root_cause: nothing enforced unique/contiguous migration numbers, so parallel branches could silently collide

cold_cases.title: EstimateStatusChip expiry tests flaked — asserted against the real clock
cold_cases.root_cause: date-relative fixtures crossed expiry boundaries depending on the day the suite ran (hermeticity violation: unfrozen clock)
